_note: Curated navigation map for omega-codedb. Each partition is a named area of the repo. An area may span several directories (dirs) and/or pin specific files via globs. omega-codedb resolves every source file to its most specific area (globs beat the longest matching directory prefix). Keep this file editorial: add areas as the repo grows, tag them with the words a searcher would type, and list the few key symbols/headers worth surfacing. The depends_on array on each partition records the *direct* logical dependencies on other areas (by name) — transitive deps are implied and should not be repeated. Edges point at what the area uses, not what uses it; reverse lookups are the consumer's job. The symbol index (codedb.py) covers everything else automatically.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Core
partitions.purpose: Foundational WTK utilities shared by every other subsystem: core types, scheduling, and base infrastructure. Start here for cross-cutting helpers.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK UI Framework
partitions.purpose: High-level application UI: window lifecycle, the View/Widget hierarchy, layout, and the app entry point. Look here to build widget-based UIs or manage windows.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Widget Library
partitions.purpose: Concrete reusable widgets: containers (HStack/VStack), primitives (Rectangle/Label/Icon/Image), user inputs (Button/TextInput/Slider/Dropdown), navigation, overlays, media widgets.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Composition Engine
partitions.purpose: GPU-accelerated composition: Views render into Layers that form a LayerTree submitted to the Compositor. Houses animation, brushes, paths, geometry, fonts, and text layout. (Backend subdirectories have their own, more specific areas.)

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Composition Backend
partitions.purpose: Platform-agnostic rendering infrastructure shared by all graphics backends: render targets, pipelines, render passes, resource pooling, glyph atlas, blur scratch. Look here for rendering bugs that aren't API-specific.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Metal Backend (macOS)
partitions.purpose: Metal/Core Image/Core Text implementation of the WTK compositor for macOS and iOS.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Direct2D Backend (Windows)
partitions.purpose: Direct2D/DirectComposition/DirectWrite implementation of the WTK compositor for Windows.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Vulkan Backend (Linux)
partitions.purpose: Vulkan/Harfbuzz implementation of the WTK compositor for Linux (X11/Wayland).

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Native Abstraction
partitions.purpose: Cross-platform OS-integration layer: native windows, events, menus, dialogs, notifications, themes. The bridge between WTK's high-level API and OS APIs. (Per-OS implementations have their own areas.)

partitions.name: OmegaWTK macOS Native Layer (Cocoa)
partitions.purpose: Cocoa/AppKit implementation of the native abstraction for macOS.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Windows Native Layer (Win32)
partitions.purpose: Win32 implementation of the native abstraction for Windows.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Linux/GTK Native Layer
partitions.purpose: GTK implementation of the native abstraction for Linux.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Mobile Native Layers
partitions.purpose: iOS (UIKit) and Android native layers for the mobile targets.

partitions.name: OmegaWTK Tests
partitions.purpose: WTK integration and unit tests (BasicAppTest, layout/composition stress tests, media/video tests).

partitions.name: OmegaGTE Public API
partitions.purpose: Public surface of the graphics & tessellation engine: device, command queues, textures, buffers, pipelines, render targets, meshes, math, the tessellation engine (TE), and raytracing types.

partitions.name: OmegaGTE Core Implementation
partitions.purpose: Backend-neutral engine runtime: GE/TE implementation glue and runtime shader compilation. (Per-API backends below have more specific areas.)

partitions.name: OmegaGTE Direct3D 12 Backend
partitions.purpose: Direct3D 12 implementation of the graphics engine for Windows.

partitions.name: OmegaGTE Metal Backend
partitions.purpose: Metal implementation of the graphics engine for macOS/iOS.

partitions.name: OmegaGTE Vulkan Backend
partitions.purpose: Vulkan implementation of the graphics engine for Linux.

partitions.name: OmegaGTE Common Backend Utilities
partitions.purpose: Helpers shared across the D3D12/Metal/Vulkan backends.

partitions.name: OmegaGTE Tests
partitions.purpose: Per-API GTE tests (2D, compute, blit, CPU/GPU tessellation, asset) under directx/metal/vulkan.

partitions.name: OmegaSL Compiler
partitions.purpose: The omegaslc shader-language compiler: full pipeline from preprocessing through lexing, parsing, semantic analysis, optimization, and code generation to HLSL/Metal/GLSL. (Frontend and codegen file-groups are pinned as finer areas below.)

partitions.name: OmegaSL Frontend
partitions.purpose: OmegaSL compiler front end specifically: preprocessing, lexing, parsing, AST construction, and semantic analysis. Pinned by file glob because every stage shares gte/omegasl/src. Use this to find where source text becomes a checked AST.

partitions.name: OmegaSL Code Generation
partitions.purpose: OmegaSL compiler back end specifically: the codegen core plus the HLSL/Metal(MSL)/GLSL target emitters, constant folding, and feature scanning. Pinned by file glob. Use this to find where the checked AST becomes backend shader source.

partitions.name: OmegaSL Tests & Samples
partitions.purpose: OmegaSL language conformance tests and example shaders — the canonical corpus of valid .omegasl across vertex/fragment/compute stages.

partitions.name: AUTOM Sources
partitions.purpose: The AUTOM native build-system generator: target model, toolchain management, execution driver, diagnostics, and host/platform support. (The build-file engine and generators have finer areas.)

partitions.name: AUTOM Build-File Engine
partitions.purpose: Parser and execution engine for AUTOM.build files: lexer, AST, AST factory, builtins, and the interpreter.

partitions.name: AUTOM Code Generators
partitions.purpose: Project-file generators that emit Ninja, Xcode, Visual Studio, Gradle, and compile_commands.json from the AUTOM target model.

partitions.name: AUTOM Standard Modules
partitions.purpose: AUTOM standard-library modules written in the build language (e.g. filesystem support).

partitions.name: AUTOM Dependency Tools
partitions.purpose: AUTOMDEPS / autom-deps: the dependency fetch-and-sync tooling that vendors third-party sources.

partitions.name: AUTOM Tests
partitions.purpose: AUTOM generator and language tests (gen-ninja, gen-sln, gen-xcode, gen-complex).

partitions.name: OmegaCommon Public API
partitions.purpose: Shared runtime/utility public headers: core type system (SharedHandle, Vector, String), and the declarations for filesystem, format, JSON, networking, threading, and assets.

partitions.name: OmegaCommon Sources
partitions.purpose: Implementations of the common runtime services: filesystem, JSON, crypto/hashing, formatting, regex, CLI parsing, networking, asset bundles, and threading (with per-OS subfolders).

partitions.name: OmegaCommon Image Codecs
partitions.purpose: PNG/JPEG/TIFF image codecs used for asset and texture loading.

partitions.name: OmegaCommon Unicode & Text
partitions.purpose: Unicode text handling: bidi paragraphs, break iteration, script-run iteration, and the UniString type.

partitions.name: omega-wrapgen
partitions.purpose: Language-binding generator: parses C++ headers and emits bindings for other languages.

partitions.name: omega-ebin
partitions.purpose: Binary-embedding tool that turns data files into linkable C++ source.

partitions.name: omegawtk-assetc
partitions.purpose: Asset-bundle compiler for OmegaWTK assets.

partitions.name: AQUA Public API
partitions.purpose: Physics/simulation engine public surface: the rigid-body World, math core, and integrator. Backend is hidden behind this API. Early-stage scaffold.

partitions.name: AQUA Sources
partitions.purpose: Simulation implementation: context, simulation spaces, and placeholder kernels.

partitions.name: AQUA Tests
partitions.purpose: AQUA unit tests.

partitions.name: Omega kREATE Public API
partitions.purpose: Game-engine public headers: engine core, scene model, and editor entry points. Builds on OmegaGTE + OmegaWTK. Early stage.

partitions.name: Omega kREATE Core
partitions.purpose: Engine implementation: core, renderer, and pipeline. (Renderer/pipeline/platform have finer areas.)

partitions.name: Omega kREATE Renderer
partitions.purpose: Internal kREATE renderer built on OmegaGTE.

partitions.name: Omega kREATE Pipeline
partitions.purpose: kREATE render/asset pipeline system.

partitions.name: Omega kREATE Platform
partitions.purpose: Per-OS platform abstraction and entry points (windows/linux/macos/ios/android).

partitions.name: Omega kREATE Tests
partitions.purpose: kREATE tests and example game.

partitions.name: OmegaVA Public API
partitions.purpose: Audio/video module public surface: media playback, decoding, and device abstraction. Backend is hidden behind this API.

partitions.name: OmegaVA Core
partitions.purpose: Backend-neutral OmegaVA core utilities. (Per-platform backends below.)

partitions.name: OmegaVA FFmpeg Backend (Linux)
partitions.purpose: FFmpeg-based audio/video backend for Linux (statically links vendored FFmpeg n7.1.1 + ALSA).

partitions.name: OmegaVA WMF Backend (Windows)
partitions.purpose: Windows Media Foundation backend for Windows.

partitions.name: OmegaVA AVFoundation Backend (Apple)
partitions.purpose: AVFoundation backend for macOS and iOS.

partitions.name: OmegaVA Android Backend
partitions.purpose: Android audio/video backend.

partitions.name: omega-codedb
partitions.purpose: This tool: the zero-dependency code locator (curated area map + auto-built symbol index) that helps agents navigate the repo without grepping.

partitions.name: Repo Documentation Tooling
partitions.purpose: Sphinx documentation infrastructure and theme assets for the project docs.
